codeReview.summary: The fix correctly moves API keys to PBKDF2+salt and adds scope checks on several routes and key-management handlers, but one original blocking scope gap remains unresolved.

issues.description: GET /spikes/:id still authorizes via validateAdminOrUserToken without a hasScope(auth, 'GET') check, so write-scoped API keys can read spike data. This does not satisfy the prior blocking finding about /spikes/:id scope enforcement. Evidence: worker/src/index.ts:86-90 plus worker/src/utils/validation.ts:52-60 (API keys are accepted by validateAdminOrUserToken).

sharedStateObservations.observation: worker-backend procedure requires manual wrangler verification and explicit deviation reporting, but this fix session reports followedProcedure=true without recording that the manual wrangler step was skipped.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Fix transcript skeleton shows vitest/tsc/eslint runs but no wrangler dev/curl manual verification commands; handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true with no deviations.

summary: Reviewed prior failure context, fix handoff/transcript, and commit d59427a (with original commit 667524c for comparison). Result: FAIL — PBKDF2 and key-management scope fixes landed, but /spikes/:id still lacks GET scope enforcement for API-key auth, leaving an original blocking scope issue unresolved.
